types.name: Attribute
types.description:
A custom property. Attributes are used to store additional information about a Shopify resource, such as
products, customers, or orders. Attributes are stored as key-value pairs.

For example, a list of attributes might include whether a customer is a first-time buyer (`"customer_first_order": "true"`),
whether an order is gift-wrapped (`"gift_wrapped": "true"`), a preferred delivery date
(`"preferred_delivery_date": "2025-10-01"`), the discount applied (`"loyalty_discount_applied": "10%"`), and any
notes provided by the customer (`"customer_notes": "Please leave at the front door"`).

fields.name: key
fields.description: The key or name of the attribute. For example, `"customer_first_order"`.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The value of the attribute. For example, `"true"`.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: Represents `true` or `false` values.

types.name: BuyerIdentity
types.description:
Information about the customer that's interacting with the cart. It includes details such as the
customer's email and phone number, and the total amount of money the customer has spent in the store.
This information helps personalize the checkout experience and ensures that accurate pricing and delivery options
are displayed to customers.

fields.name: customer
fields.description: The [customer](https://help.shopify.com/manual/customers/manage-customers) that's interacting with the cart.

fields.name: email
fields.description: The email address of the customer that's interacting with the cart.

fields.name: isAuthenticated
fields.description:
Whether the customer is authenticated through their
[customer account](https://help.shopify.com/manual/customers/customer-accounts).

fields.name: phone
fields.description: The phone number of the customer that's interacting with the cart.

fields.name: purchasingCompany
fields.description:
The company of a B2B customer that's interacting with the cart.
Used to manage and track purchases made by businesses rather than individual customers.

types.name: Cart
types.description:
The cart where the Function is running. A cart contains the merchandise that a customer intends to purchase
and information about the customer, such as the customer's email address and phone number.

fields.name: attribute
fields.description:
The custom attributes associated with a cart to store additional information. Cart attributes
allow you to collect specific information from customers on the **Cart** page, such as order notes,
gift wrapping requests, or custom product details. Attributes are stored as key-value pairs.

args.name: key
args.description: The key of the cart attribute to retrieve. For example, `"gift_wrapping"`.

fields.name: buyerIdentity
fields.description:
Information about the customer that's interacting with the cart. It includes details such as the
customer's email and phone number, and the total amount of money the customer has spent in the store.
This information helps personalize the checkout experience and ensures that accurate pricing and delivery options
are displayed to customers.

fields.name: cost
fields.description:
A breakdown of the costs that the customer will pay at checkout. It includes the total amount,
the subtotal before taxes and duties, the tax amount, and duty charges.

fields.name: deliverableLines
fields.description: The items in a cart that are eligible for fulfillment and can be delivered to the customer.

fields.name: deliveryGroups
fields.description:
A collection of items that are grouped by shared delivery characteristics. Delivery groups streamline
fulfillment by organizing items that can be shipped together, based on the customer's
shipping address. For example, if a customer orders a t-shirt and a pair of shoes that can be shipped
together, then the items are included in the same delivery group.

In the [Order Discount](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/functions/reference/order-discounts) and
[Product Discount](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/functions/reference/product-discounts) legacy APIs,
the `cart.deliveryGroups` input is always an empty array. This means you can't access delivery groups when
creating Order Discount or Product Discount Functions. If you need to apply discounts to shipping costs,
then use the [Discount Function API](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/functions/reference/discount)
instead.

fields.name: lines
fields.description:
The items in a cart that the customer intends to purchase. A cart line is an entry in the
customer's cart that represents a single unit of a product variant. For example, if a customer adds two
different sizes of the same t-shirt to their cart, then each size is represented as a separate cart line.

fields.name: localizedFields
fields.description:
The additional fields on the **Cart** page that are required for international orders in specific countries,
such as customs information or tax identification numbers.

args.name: keys
args.description: The keys of the localized fields to retrieve.

fields.name: metafield
fields.description:
A [custom field](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that stores additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

args.name: namespace
args.description:
A category that organizes a group of metafields. Namespaces are used to prevent naming conflicts
between different apps or different parts of the same app. If omitted, then the
[app-reserved namespace](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/ownership)
is used.

args.name: key
args.description:
The unique identifier for the metafield within its namespace. A metafield is composed of a
namespace and a key, in the format `namespace.key`.

fields.name: retailLocation
fields.description: The physical location where a retail order is created or completed.

types.name: CartCost
types.description:
A breakdown of the costs that the customer will pay at checkout. It includes the total amount,
the subtotal before taxes and duties, the tax amount, and duty charges.

fields.name: subtotalAmount
fields.description: The amount, before taxes and cart-level discounts, for the customer to pay.

fields.name: totalAmount
fields.description: The total amount for the customer to pay at checkout.

fields.name: totalDutyAmount
fields.description: The duty charges for a customer to pay at checkout.

fields.name: totalTaxAmount
fields.description: The total tax amount for the customer to pay at checkout.

types.name: CartDeliveryGroup
types.description:
Information about items in a cart that are grouped by shared delivery characteristics.
Delivery groups streamline fulfillment by organizing items that can be shipped together, based on the customer's
shipping address. For example, if a customer orders a t-shirt and a pair of shoes that can be shipped
together, then the items are included in the same delivery group.

fields.name: cartLines
fields.description:
Information about items in a cart that a customer intends to purchase. A cart line is an entry in the
customer's cart that represents a single unit of a product variant. For example, if a customer adds two
different sizes of the same t-shirt to their cart, then each size is represented as a separate cart line.

fields.name: deliveryAddress
fields.description: The shipping or destination address associated with the delivery group.

fields.name: deliveryOptions
fields.description:
The delivery options available for the delivery group. Delivery options are the different ways that customers
can choose to have their orders shipped. Examples include express shipping or standard shipping.

fields.name: groupType
fields.description: The type of merchandise in the delivery group.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
A [globally-unique ID](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/usage/gids)
for the delivery group.

fields.name: selectedDeliveryOption
fields.description: Information about the delivery option that the customer has selected.

types.name: CartDeliveryGroupType
types.description: Defines what type of merchandise is in the delivery group.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: The delivery group only contains subscription merchandise.

enumValues.name: ONE_TIME_PURCHASE
enumValues.description:
The delivery group only contains merchandise that is either a one time purchase or a first delivery of
subscription merchandise.

types.name: CartDeliveryOption
types.description:
Information about a delivery option that's available for an item in a cart. Delivery options are the different
ways that customers can choose to have their orders shipped. Examples include express shipping or standard
shipping.

fields.name: code
fields.description:
A unique identifier that represents the delivery option offered to customers.
For example, `Canada Post Expedited`.

fields.name: cost
fields.description: The amount that the customer pays if they select the delivery option.

fields.name: deliveryMethodType
fields.description:
The delivery method associated with the delivery option. A delivery method is a way that merchants can
fulfill orders from their online stores. Delivery methods include shipping to an address,
[local pickup](https://help.shopify.com/manual/fulfillment/setup/delivery-methods/pickup-in-store),
and shipping to a [pickup point](https://help.shopify.com/manual/fulfillment/shopify-shipping/pickup-points),
all of which are natively supported by Shopify checkout.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A single-line description of the delivery option, with HTML tags removed.

fields.name: handle
fields.description:
A unique, human-readable identifier of the delivery option's title.
A handle can contain letters, hyphens (`-`), and numbers, but not spaces.
For example, `standard-shipping`.

fields.name: title
fields.description:
The name of the delivery option that displays to customers. The title is used to construct the delivery
option's handle. For example, if a delivery option is titled "Standard Shipping", then the handle is
`standard-shipping`.

types.name: CartDeliveryOptionsTransformRunResult
types.description:
The output of the Function run target.
The object contains the operations to apply to delivery options in checkout.

inputFields.name: operations
inputFields.description:
The ordered list of operations to apply to the list of
[delivery options](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/checkout/delivery-shipping/delivery-options/build-function).

types.name: CartLine
types.description:
Information about an item in a cart that a customer intends to purchase. A cart line is an entry in the
customer's cart that represents a single unit of a product variant. For example, if a customer adds two
different sizes of the same t-shirt to their cart, then each size is represented as a separate cart line.

fields.name: attribute
fields.description:
The custom attributes associated with a cart to store additional information. Cart attributes
allow you to collect specific information from customers on the **Cart** page, such as order notes,
gift wrapping requests, or custom product details. Attributes are stored as key-value pairs.

Cart line attributes are equivalent to the
[`line_item`](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/purchase-options/subscriptions/selling-plans)
object in Liquid.

args.name: key
args.description: The key of the cart attribute to retrieve. For example, `"gift_wrapping"`.

fields.name: cost
fields.description:
The cost of an item in a cart that the customer intends to purchase. Cart lines are entries in the customer's
cart that represent a single unit of a product variant. For example, if a customer adds two different sizes of
the same t-shirt to their cart, then each size is represented as a separate cart line.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the cart line.

fields.name: merchandise
fields.description: The item that the customer intends to purchase.

fields.name: parentRelationship
fields.description:
The [nested relationship](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/product-merchandising/nested-cart-lines)
between this line and its parent line, if any.

fields.name: quantity
fields.description: The quantity of the item that the customer intends to purchase.

fields.name: sellingPlanAllocation
fields.description:
The [selling plan](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/purchase-options/subscriptions/selling-plans)
associated with the cart line, including information about how a product variant can be sold and purchased.

types.name: CartLineCost
types.description:
The cost of an item in a cart that the customer intends to purchase. Cart lines are entries in the customer's
cart that represent a single unit of a product variant. For example, if a customer adds two different sizes of
the same t-shirt to their cart, then each size is represented as a separate cart line.

fields.name: amountPerQuantity
fields.description:
The cost of a single unit. For example, if a customer purchases three units of a product
that are priced at $10 each, then the `amountPerQuantity` is $10.

fields.name: compareAtAmountPerQuantity
fields.description:
The `compareAt` price of a single unit before any discounts are applied. This field is used to calculate and display
savings for customers. For example, if a product's `compareAtAmountPerQuantity` is $25 and its current price
is $20, then the customer sees a $5 discount. This value can change based on the buyer's identity and is
`null` when the value is hidden from buyers.

fields.name: subtotalAmount
fields.description:
The cost of items in the cart before applying any discounts to certain items.
This amount serves as the starting point for calculating any potential savings customers
might receive through promotions or discounts.

fields.name: totalAmount
fields.description: The total cost of items in a cart.

types.name: CartLineParentRelationship
types.description: Represents the relationship between a cart line and its parent line.

fields.name: parent
fields.description: The parent line in the relationship.

types.name: CollectionMembership
types.description:
Whether the product is in the specified collection.

A collection is a group of products that can be displayed in online stores and other sales channels in
categories, which makes it easy for customers to find them. For example, an athletics store might create
different collections for running attire and accessories.

fields.name: collectionId
fields.description:
A [globally-unique ID](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/usage/gids)
for the collection.

fields.name: isMember
fields.description: Whether the product is in the specified collection.

types.name: Company
types.description: Represents information about a company which is also a customer of the shop.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The date and time ([ISO 8601 format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)) at which the company was created in Shopify.

fields.name: externalId
fields.description: A unique externally-supplied ID for the company.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the company.

fields.name: metafield
fields.description:
A [custom field](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that stores additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

args.name: namespace
args.description:
A category that organizes a group of metafields. Namespaces are used to prevent naming conflicts
between different apps or different parts of the same app. If omitted, then the
[app-reserved namespace](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/ownership)
is used.

args.name: key
args.description:
The unique identifier for the metafield within its namespace. A metafield is composed of a
namespace and a key, in the format `namespace.key`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the company.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description: The date and time ([ISO 8601 format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)) at which the company was last modified.

types.name: CompanyContact
types.description: A company's main point of contact.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The date and time ([ISO 8601 format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)) at which the company contact was created in Shopify.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the company.

fields.name: locale
fields.description: The company contact's locale (language).

fields.name: title
fields.description: The company contact's job title.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description: The date and time ([ISO 8601 format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)) at which the company contact was last modified.

types.name: CompanyLocation
types.description: A company's location.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The date and time ([ISO 8601 format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)) at which the company location was created in Shopify.

fields.name: externalId
fields.description: A unique externally-supplied ID for the company.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the company.

fields.name: locale
fields.description: The preferred locale of the company location.

fields.name: metafield
fields.description:
A [custom field](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that stores additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

args.name: namespace
args.description:
A category that organizes a group of metafields. Namespaces are used to prevent naming conflicts
between different apps or different parts of the same app. If omitted, then the
[app-reserved namespace](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/ownership)
is used.

args.name: key
args.description:
The unique identifier for the metafield within its namespace. A metafield is composed of a
namespace and a key, in the format `namespace.key`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the company location.

fields.name: ordersCount
fields.description: The number of orders placed at this company location.

fields.name: totalSpent
fields.description: The total amount spent at this company location.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description: The date and time ([ISO 8601 format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)) at which the company location was last modified.

types.name: Country
types.description:
The country for which the store is customized, reflecting local preferences and regulations.
Localization might influence the language, currency, and product offerings available in a store to enhance
the shopping experience for customers in that region.

fields.name: isoCode
fields.description: The ISO code of the country.

types.name: CountryCode
types.description:
The code designating a country/region, which generally follows ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 guidelines.
If a territory doesn't have a country code value in the `CountryCode` enum, then it might be considered a subdivision
of another country. For example, the territories associated with Spain are represented by the country code `ES`,
and the territories associated with the United States of America are represented by the country code `US`.

types.name: CurrencyCode
types.description:
The currency codes that represent the world currencies throughout the Admin API. Currency codes include
[standard ISO 4217 codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217), legacy codes, non-standard codes,
digital currency codes.

types.name: CustomProduct
types.description:
A custom product represents a product that doesn't map to Shopify's
[standard product categories](https://help.shopify.com/manual/products/details/product-type).
For example, you can use a custom product to manage gift cards, shipping requirements, localized product
information, or weight measurements and conversions.

fields.name: isGiftCard
fields.description: Whether the merchandise is a gift card.

fields.name: requiresShipping
fields.description:
Whether the item needs to be shipped to the customer. For example, a
digital gift card doesn't need to be shipped, but a t-shirt does
need to be shipped.

fields.name: title
fields.description:
The localized name for the product that displays to customers. The title is used to construct the product's
handle, which is a unique, human-readable string of the product's title. For example, if a product is titled
"Black Sunglasses", then the handle is `black-sunglasses`.

fields.name: weight
fields.description: The product variant's weight, in the system of measurement set in the `weightUnit` field.

fields.name: weightUnit
fields.description: The unit of measurement for weight.

types.name: Customer
types.description:
Represents a [customer](https://help.shopify.com/manual/customers/manage-customers).
`Customer` returns data including the customer's contact information and order history.

fields.name: amountSpent
fields.description:
The total amount that the customer has spent on orders.
The amount is converted from the shop's currency to the currency of the cart using a market rate.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description:
The full name of the customer, based on the values for `firstName` and `lastName`.
If `firstName` and `lastName` aren't specified, then the value is the customer's email address.
If the email address isn't specified, then the value is the customer's phone number.

fields.name: email
fields.description: The customer's email address.

fields.name: firstName
fields.description: The customer's first name.

fields.name: hasAnyTag
fields.description:
Whether the customer is associated with any of the specified tags. The customer must have at least one tag
from the list to return `true`.

args.name: tags
args.description:
A comma-separated list of searchable keywords that are associated with the customer. For example,
`"VIP, Gold"` returns customers with either the `VIP` or `Gold` tag.

fields.name: hasTags
fields.description: Whether the customer is associated with the specified tags.

args.name: tags
args.description:
A comma-separated list of searchable keywords that are associated with the customer. For example,
`"VIP, Gold"` returns customers with both the `VIP` and `Gold` tags.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
A [globally-unique ID](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/usage/gids)
for the customer.

fields.name: lastName
fields.description: The customer's last name.

fields.name: metafield
fields.description:
A [custom field](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that stores additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

args.name: namespace
args.description:
A category that organizes a group of metafields. Namespaces are used to prevent naming conflicts
between different apps or different parts of the same app. If omitted, then the
[app-reserved namespace](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/ownership)
is used.

args.name: key
args.description:
The unique identifier for the metafield within its namespace. A metafield is composed of a
namespace and a key, in the format `namespace.key`.

fields.name: numberOfOrders
fields.description: The total number of orders that the customer has made at the store.

types.name: Date
types.description:
Represents an [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)-encoded date string.
For example, September 7, 2019 is represented as `"2019-07-16"`.

types.name: DateTime
types.description:
Represents an [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)-encoded date and time string.
For example, 3:50 pm on September 7, 2019 in the time zone of UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) is
represented as `"2019-09-07T15:50:00Z`".

types.name: DateTimeWithoutTimezone
types.description:
A subset of the [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format that includes the date and time but not the timezone which is determined from context.

For example, "2018-01-01T00:00:00".

types.name: Decimal
types.description:
A signed decimal number, which supports arbitrary precision and is serialized as a string.

Example values: `"29.99"`, `"29.999"`.

types.name: DeliverableCartLine
types.description: Represents information about the merchandise in the cart.

fields.name: attribute
fields.description:
The custom attributes associated with a cart to store additional information. Cart attributes
allow you to collect specific information from customers on the **Cart** page, such as order notes,
gift wrapping requests, or custom product details. Attributes are stored as key-value pairs.

Cart line attributes are equivalent to the
[`line_item`](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/purchase-options/subscriptions/selling-plans)
object in Liquid.

args.name: key
args.description: The key of the cart attribute to retrieve. For example, `"gift_wrapping"`.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the cart line.

fields.name: merchandise
fields.description: The item that the customer intends to purchase.

fields.name: quantity
fields.description: The quantity of the item that the customer intends to purchase.

types.name: DeliveryCustomization
types.description: A customization representing how delivery options will be ordered, hidden, or renamed.

fields.name: metafield
fields.description:
A [custom field](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that stores additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

args.name: namespace
args.description:
A category that organizes a group of metafields. Namespaces are used to prevent naming conflicts
between different apps or different parts of the same app. If omitted, then the
[app-reserved namespace](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/ownership)
is used.

args.name: key
args.description:
The unique identifier for the metafield within its namespace. A metafield is composed of a
namespace and a key, in the format `namespace.key`.

types.name: DeliveryMethod
types.description: List of different delivery method types.

types.name: DeliveryOptionHideOperation
types.description: An operation that hides a delivery option from a list that's offered to customers at checkout.

inputFields.name: deliveryOptionHandle
inputFields.description: The handle of the delivery option to hide.

types.name: DeliveryOptionMoveOperation
types.description:
An operation that sorts a list of delivery options that are offered to customers at checkout.

If you reorder shipping delivery options, then you are
[prohibited](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/launch/app-requirements-checklist#prohibited-app-types).
from automatically selecting higher-priced delivery alternatives by default. The cheapest shipping delivery option
must always be the first option selected.

inputFields.name: deliveryOptionHandle
inputFields.description: The handle of the delivery option to move.

inputFields.name: index
inputFields.description: The target index within the delivery group to move the delivery option to.

types.name: DeliveryOptionRenameOperation
types.description:
An operation that renames a delivery option that's offered to customers at checkout.

The carrier name is automatically prepended to the delivery option title at checkout when using the
`DeliveryOptionRenameOperation` object, and can't be altered or omitted through the API. For example, if the carrier name
is **UPS** and the option is **Standard**, then you could change **UPS Standard** to **UPS Standard Shipping**,
but you couldn't change **UPS Standard** to **Standard Shipping**.

inputFields.name: deliveryOptionHandle
inputFields.description: The handle of the delivery option to rename.

inputFields.name: title
inputFields.description: The new name for the delivery option.

types.name: DeprecatedOperation
types.description: An operation to apply to the list of [delivery options](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/checkout/delivery-shipping/delivery-options/build-function).

types.name: Float
types.description: Represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.name: FunctionResult
types.description:
The output of the Function run target.
The object contains the operations to apply to delivery options in checkout. In API versions 2023-10 and beyond, this type is deprecated in favor of `FunctionRunResult`.

inputFields.name: operations
inputFields.description:
The ordered list of operations to apply to the list of
[delivery options](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/checkout/delivery-shipping/delivery-options/build-function).

types.name: FunctionRunResult
types.description:
The output of the Function run target.
The object contains the operations to apply to delivery options in checkout.

inputFields.name: operations
inputFields.description:
The ordered list of operations to apply to the list of
[delivery options](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/checkout/delivery-shipping/delivery-options/build-function).

types.name: Handle
types.description:
A function-scoped handle to a refer a resource.
The Handle type appears in a JSON response as a String, but it is not intended to be human-readable.
Example value: `"10079785100"`

types.name: HasMetafields
types.description: Represents information about the metafields associated to the specified resource.

fields.name: metafield
fields.description:
A [custom field](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that stores additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

args.name: namespace
args.description:
A category that organizes a group of metafields. Namespaces are used to prevent naming conflicts
between different apps or different parts of the same app. If omitted, then the
[app-reserved namespace](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/ownership)
is used.

args.name: key
args.description:
The unique identifier for the metafield within its namespace. A metafield is composed of a
namespace and a key, in the format `namespace.key`.

types.name: HasTagResponse
types.description: Whether a Shopify resource, such as a product or customer, has a specified tag.

fields.name: hasTag
fields.description: Whether the Shopify resource has the tag.

fields.name: tag
fields.description:
A searchable keyword that's associated with a Shopify resource, such as a product or customer. For example,
a merchant might apply the `sports` and `summer` tags to products that are associated with sportswear for
summer.

types.name: HideOperation
types.description: An operation that hides a delivery option from a list that's offered to customers at checkout.

inputFields.name: deliveryOptionHandle
inputFields.description: The handle of the delivery option to hide.

types.name: ID
types.description:
Represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object.
The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String, but it is not intended to be human-readable.

Example value: `"gid://shopify/Product/10079785100"`

fields.name: cart
fields.description:
The cart where the Function is running. A cart contains the merchandise that a customer intends to purchase
and information about the customer, such as the customer's email address and phone number.

fields.name: deliveryCustomization
fields.description:
The backend logic that the Function is running to define how
[delivery options](https://shopify.dev/apps/build/checkout/delivery-shipping/delivery-options/build-function)
are sorted, hidden, or renamed. It includes the
[metafields](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data)
that are associated with the customization.

fields.name: localization
fields.description:
The regional and language settings that determine how the Function
handles currency, numbers, dates, and other locale-specific values
during discount calculations. These settings are based on the store's configured
[localization practices](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/localization-practices-shopify-functions).

fields.name: presentmentCurrencyRate
fields.description:
The exchange rate used to convert discounts between the shop's default
currency and the currency that displays to the customer during checkout.
For example, if a store operates in USD but a customer is viewing discounts in EUR,
then the presentment currency rate handles this conversion for accurate pricing.

fields.name: shop
fields.description:
Information about the shop where the Function is running, including the shop's timezone
setting and associated [metafields](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data).

types.name: Int
types.description: Represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: JSON
Language
types.description:
The language for which the store is customized, ensuring content is tailored to local customers.
This includes product descriptions and customer communications that resonate with the target audience.

fields.name: isoCode
fields.description: The ISO code.

types.name: LanguageCode
types.description: Language codes supported by Shopify.

types.name: LocalTime
types.description:
The current time based on the
[store's timezone setting](https://help.shopify.com/manual/intro-to-shopify/initial-setup/setup-business-settings).

fields.name: date
fields.description: The current date relative to the parent object.

fields.name: dateTimeAfter
fields.description: Returns true if the current date and time is at or past the given date and time, and false otherwise.

args.name: dateTime
args.description: The date and time to compare against, assumed to be in the timezone of the parent object.

fields.name: dateTimeBefore
fields.description: Returns true if the current date and time is before the given date and time, and false otherwise.

args.name: dateTime
args.description: The date and time to compare against, assumed to be in the timezone of the parent timezone.

fields.name: dateTimeBetween
fields.description: Returns true if the current date and time is between the two given date and times, and false otherwise.

args.name: startDateTime
args.description: The lower bound time to compare against, assumed to be in the timezone of the parent timezone.

args.name: endDateTime
args.description: The upper bound time to compare against, assumed to be in the timezone of the parent timezone.

fields.name: timeAfter
fields.description: Returns true if the current time is at or past the given time, and false otherwise.

args.name: time
args.description: The time to compare against, assumed to be in the timezone of the parent timezone.

fields.name: timeBefore
fields.description: Returns true if the current time is at or past the given time, and false otherwise.

args.name: time
args.description: The time to compare against, assumed to be in the timezone of the parent timezone.

fields.name: timeBetween
fields.description: Returns true if the current time is between the two given times, and false otherwise.

args.name: startTime
args.description: The lower bound time to compare against, assumed to be in the timezone of the parent timezone.

args.name: endTime
args.description: The upper bound time to compare against, assumed to be in the timezone of the parent timezone.

types.name: Localization
types.description:
Details about the localized experience for the store in a specific region, including country and language
settings. The localized experience is determined by the store's settings and the customer's location.
Localization ensures that customers can access relevant content and options while browsing or purchasing
products in a store.

fields.name: country
fields.description:
The country for which the store is customized, reflecting local preferences and regulations.
Localization might influence the language, currency, and product offerings available in a store to enhance
the shopping experience for customers in that region.

fields.name: language
fields.description:
The language for which the store is customized, ensuring content is tailored to local customers.
This includes product descriptions and customer communications that resonate with the target audience.

fields.name: market
fields.description: The market of the active localized experience.
fields.deprecationReason: This `market` field will be removed in a future version of the API.

types.name: LocalizedField
types.description: Represents the value captured by a localized field. Localized fields are additional fields required by certain countries on international orders. For example, some countries require additional fields for customs information or tax identification numbers.

fields.name: key
fields.description: The key of the localized field.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The title of the localized field.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The value of the localized field.

types.name: LocalizedFieldKey
types.description: Unique key identifying localized fields.

types.name: Location
types.description: Represents the location where the inventory resides.

fields.name: address
fields.description: The address of this location.

fields.name: handle
fields.description: The location handle.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The location id.

fields.name: metafield
fields.description:
A [custom field](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that stores additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

args.name: namespace
args.description:
A category that organizes a group of metafields. Namespaces are used to prevent naming conflicts
between different apps or different parts of the same app. If omitted, then the
[app-reserved namespace](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/ownership)
is used.

args.name: key
args.description:
The unique identifier for the metafield within its namespace. A metafield is composed of a
namespace and a key, in the format `namespace.key`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the location.

types.name: LocationAddress
types.description: Represents the address of a location.

fields.name: address1
fields.description: The first line of the address for the location.

fields.name: address2
fields.description: The second line of the address for the location.

fields.name: city
fields.description: The city of the location.

fields.name: country
fields.description: The country of the location.

fields.name: countryCode
fields.description: The country code of the location.

fields.name: formatted
fields.description: A formatted version of the address for the location.

fields.name: latitude
fields.description: The approximate latitude coordinates of the location.

fields.name: longitude
fields.description: The approximate longitude coordinates of the location.

fields.name: phone
fields.description: The phone number of the location.

fields.name: province
fields.description: The province of the location.

fields.name: provinceCode
fields.description: The code for the province, state, or district of the address of the location.

fields.name: zip
fields.description: The ZIP code of the location.

types.name: MailingAddress
types.description: Represents a mailing address.

fields.name: address1
fields.description: The first line of the address. Typically the street address or PO Box number.

fields.name: address2
fields.description: The second line of the address. Typically the number of the apartment, suite, or unit.

fields.name: city
fields.description: The name of the city, district, village, or town.

fields.name: company
fields.description: The name of the customer's company or organization.

fields.name: countryCode
fields.description: The two-letter code for the country of the address. For example, US.

fields.name: firstName
fields.description: The first name of the customer.

fields.name: lastName
fields.description: The last name of the customer.

fields.name: latitude
fields.description: The approximate latitude of the address.

fields.name: longitude
fields.description: The approximate longitude of the address.

fields.name: market
fields.description: The market of the address.
fields.deprecationReason: This `market` field will be removed in a future version of the API.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The full name of the customer, based on firstName and lastName.

fields.name: phone
fields.description: A unique phone number for the customer. Formatted using E.164 standard. For example, +16135551111.

fields.name: provinceCode
fields.description: The alphanumeric code for the region. For example, ON.

fields.name: zip
fields.description: The zip or postal code of the address.

types.name: Market
types.description:
A market is a group of one or more regions that you want to target for international sales.
By creating a market, you can configure a distinct, localized shopping experience for
customers from a specific area of the world. For example, you can
[change currency](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/current/mutations/marketCurrencySettingsUpdate),
[configure international pricing](https://shopify.dev/api/examples/product-price-lists),
or [add market-specific domains or subfolders](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/current/objects/MarketWebPresence).

fields.name: handle
fields.description: A human-readable unique string for the market automatically generated from its title.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A globally-unique identifier.

fields.name: metafield
fields.description:
A [custom field](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that stores additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

args.name: namespace
args.description:
A category that organizes a group of metafields. Namespaces are used to prevent naming conflicts
between different apps or different parts of the same app. If omitted, then the
[app-reserved namespace](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/ownership)
is used.

args.name: key
args.description:
The unique identifier for the metafield within its namespace. A metafield is composed of a
namespace and a key, in the format `namespace.key`.

fields.name: regions
fields.description: A geographic region which comprises a market.

types.name: MarketRegion
types.description: Represents a region.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the region in the language of the current localization.

types.name: MarketRegionCountry
types.description: A country which comprises a market.

fields.name: code
fields.description: The two-letter code for the country.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The country name in the language of the current localization.

types.name: Merchandise
types.description:
The item that a customer intends to purchase. Merchandise can be a product variant or a custom
product.

A product variant is a specific version of a product that comes in more than one option, such as size or color.
For example, if a merchant sells t-shirts with options for size and color, then a small, blue t-shirt would be
one product variant and a large, blue t-shirt would be another.

A custom product represents a product that doesn't map to Shopify's
[standard product categories](https://help.shopify.com/manual/products/details/product-type).
For example, you can use a custom product to manage gift cards, shipping requirements, localized product
information, or weight measurements and conversions.

types.name: Metafield
types.description:
[Custom fields](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that store additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

fields.name: jsonValue
fields.description: The data that's stored in the metafield, using JSON format.

fields.name: type
fields.description:
The [type of data](https://shopify.dev/apps/metafields/types) that the metafield stores in
the `value` field.

fields.name: value
fields.description:
The data that's stored in the metafield. The data is always stored as a string,
regardless of the [metafield's type](https://shopify.dev/apps/metafields/types).

types.name: MoneyV2
types.description: A precise monetary value and its associated currency. Combines a decimal amount with a three-letter currency code to express prices, costs, and other financial values throughout the API. For example, 12.99 USD.

fields.name: amount
fields.description:
A monetary value in decimal format, allowing for precise representation of cents or fractional
currency. For example, 12.99.

fields.name: currencyCode
fields.description:
The three-letter currency code that represents a world currency used in a store. Currency codes
include standard [standard ISO 4217 codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217), legacy codes,
and non-standard codes. For example, USD.

types.name: MoveOperation
types.description:
An operation that sorts a list of delivery options that are offered to customers at checkout.

If you reorder shipping delivery options, then you are
[prohibited](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/launch/app-requirements-checklist#prohibited-app-types).
from automatically selecting higher-priced delivery alternatives by default. The cheapest shipping delivery option
must always be the first option selected.

inputFields.name: deliveryOptionHandle
inputFields.description: The handle of the delivery option to move.

inputFields.name: index
inputFields.description: The target index within the delivery group to move the delivery option to.

types.name: MutationRoot
types.description: The root mutation for the API.

fields.name: cartDeliveryOptionsTransformRun
fields.description: Handles the Function result for the cart.delivery-options.transform.run target.

args.name: result
args.description: The result of the Function.

fields.name: handleResult
fields.description: Handles the Function result.

args.name: result
args.description: The result of the Function.

fields.name: run
fields.description: Handles the Function result for the purchase.delivery-customization.run target.

args.name: result
args.description: The result of the Function.

types.name: Operation
types.description: An operation to apply to the list of [delivery options](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/checkout/delivery-shipping/delivery-options/build-function).

inputFields.name: deliveryOptionMove
inputFields.description:
An operation that sorts a list of delivery options that are offered to customers at checkout.

If you reorder shipping delivery options, then you are
[prohibited](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/launch/app-requirements-checklist#prohibited-app-types).
from automatically selecting higher-priced delivery alternatives by default. The cheapest shipping delivery option
must always be the first option selected.

inputFields.name: deliveryOptionRename
inputFields.description:
An operation that renames a delivery option that's offered to customers at checkout.

The carrier name is automatically prepended to the delivery option title at checkout when using the
`DeliveryOptionRenameOperation` object, and can't be altered or omitted through the API. For example, if the carrier name
is **UPS** and the option is **Standard**, then you could change **UPS Standard** to **UPS Standard Shipping**,
but you couldn't change **UPS Standard** to **Standard Shipping**.

inputFields.name: deliveryOptionHide
inputFields.description: An operation that hides a delivery option from a list that's offered to customers at checkout.

types.name: Product
types.description:
The goods and services that merchants offer to customers. Products can include details such as
title, vendor, and custom data stored in [metafields](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data).
Products can be organized by grouping them into a collection.

Learn more about [managing products in a merchant's store](https://help.shopify.com/manual/products).

fields.name: handle
fields.description:
A unique, human-readable string of the product's title. A handle can contain letters, hyphens (`-`), and
numbers, but not spaces. The handle is used in the online store URL for the product. For example, if a product
is titled "Black Sunglasses", then the handle is `black-sunglasses`.

fields.name: hasAnyTag
fields.description:
Whether the product is associated with any of the specified tags. The product must have at least one tag
from the list to return `true`.

args.name: tags
args.description:
A comma-separated list of searchable keywords that are associated with the product. For example,
`"sports, summer"` returns products with either the `sports` or `summer` tag.

fields.name: hasTags
fields.description: Whether the product is associated with the specified tags.

args.name: tags
args.description:
A comma-separated list of searchable keywords that are associated with the product. For example,
`"sports, summer"` returns products with both the `sports` and `summer` tags.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
A [globally-unique ID](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/usage/gids)
for the product.

fields.name: inAnyCollection
fields.description:
Whether the product is in any of the specified collections. The product must be in at least one collection
from the list to return `true`.

A collection is a group of products that can be displayed in online stores and other sales channels in
categories, which makes it easy for customers to find them. For example, an athletics store might create
different collections for running attire and accessories.

args.name: ids
args.description:
A comma-separated list of [globally-unique collection IDs](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/usage/gids)
that are associated with the product. For example, `gid://shopify/Collection/123`, `gid://shopify/Collection/456`.

fields.name: inCollections
fields.description:
Whether the product is in the specified collections. The product must be in all of the collections in the
list to return `true`.

A collection is a group of products that can be displayed in online stores and other sales channels in
categories, which makes it easy for customers to find them. For example, an athletics store might create
different collections for running attire and accessories.

args.name: ids
args.description:
A comma-separated list of [globally-unique collection IDs](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/usage/gids)
that are associated with the product. For example, `gid://shopify/Collection/123`, `gid://shopify/Collection/456`.

fields.name: isGiftCard
fields.description: Whether the product is a gift card.

fields.name: metafield
fields.description:
A [custom field](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that stores additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

args.name: namespace
args.description:
A category that organizes a group of metafields. Namespaces are used to prevent naming conflicts
between different apps or different parts of the same app. If omitted, then the
[app-reserved namespace](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/ownership)
is used.

args.name: key
args.description:
The unique identifier for the metafield within its namespace. A metafield is composed of a
namespace and a key, in the format `namespace.key`.

fields.name: productType
fields.description:
A custom category for a product. Product types allow merchants to define categories other than the
ones available in Shopify's
[standard product categories](https://help.shopify.com/manual/products/details/product-type).

fields.name: title
fields.description:
The localized name for the product that displays to customers. The title is used to construct the product's
handle, which is a unique, human-readable string of the product's title. For example, if a product is titled
"Black Sunglasses", then the handle is `black-sunglasses`.

fields.name: vendor
fields.description: The name of the product's vendor.

types.name: ProductVariant
types.description:
A specific version of a product that comes in more than one option, such as size or color. For example,
if a merchant sells t-shirts with options for size and color, then a small, blue t-shirt would be one
product variant and a large, blue t-shirt would be another.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
A [globally-unique ID](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/usage/gids)
for the product variant.

fields.name: metafield
fields.description:
A [custom field](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that stores additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

args.name: namespace
args.description:
A category that organizes a group of metafields. Namespaces are used to prevent naming conflicts
between different apps or different parts of the same app. If omitted, then the
[app-reserved namespace](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/ownership)
is used.

args.name: key
args.description:
The unique identifier for the metafield within its namespace. A metafield is composed of a
namespace and a key, in the format `namespace.key`.

fields.name: product
fields.description:
The product associated with the product variant. For example, if a
merchant sells t-shirts with options for size and color, then a small,
blue t-shirt would be one product variant and a large, blue t-shirt would be another.
The product associated with the product variant would be the t-shirt itself.

fields.name: requiresShipping
fields.description:
Whether the item needs to be shipped to the customer. For example, a
digital gift card doesn't need to be shipped, but a t-shirt does
need to be shipped.

fields.name: sku
fields.description:
A case-sensitive identifier for the product variant in the merchant's store. For example, `"BBC-1"`.
A product variant must have a SKU to be connected to a
[fulfillment service](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/fulfillment-service-apps/build-for-fulfillment-services).

fields.name: title
fields.description: The localized name for the product variant that displays to customers.

fields.name: weight
fields.description: The product variant's weight, in the system of measurement set in the `weightUnit` field.

fields.name: weightUnit
fields.description: The unit of measurement for weight.

types.name: PurchasingCompany
types.description:
The company of a B2B customer that's interacting with the cart.
Used to manage and track purchases made by businesses rather than individual customers.

fields.name: company
fields.description: The company associated to the order or draft order.

fields.name: contact
fields.description: The company contact associated to the order or draft order.

fields.name: location
fields.description: The company location associated to the order or draft order.

types.name: RenameOperation
types.description:
An operation that renames a delivery option that's offered to customers at checkout.

The carrier name is automatically prepended to the delivery option title at checkout when using the
`RenameOperation` object, and can't be altered or omitted through the API. For example, if the carrier name
is **UPS** and the option is **Standard**, then you could change **UPS Standard** to **UPS Standard Shipping**,
but you couldn't change **UPS Standard** to **Standard Shipping**.

inputFields.name: deliveryOptionHandle
inputFields.description: The handle of the delivery option to rename.

inputFields.name: title
inputFields.description: The new name for the delivery option.

types.name: SellingPlan
types.description: Represents how products and variants can be sold and purchased.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the selling plan.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A globally-unique identifier.

fields.name: metafield
fields.description:
A [custom field](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that stores additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

args.name: namespace
args.description:
A category that organizes a group of metafields. Namespaces are used to prevent naming conflicts
between different apps or different parts of the same app. If omitted, then the
[app-reserved namespace](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/ownership)
is used.

args.name: key
args.description:
The unique identifier for the metafield within its namespace. A metafield is composed of a
namespace and a key, in the format `namespace.key`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the selling plan. For example, '6 weeks of prepaid granola, delivered weekly'.

fields.name: recurringDeliveries
fields.description: Whether purchasing the selling plan will result in multiple deliveries.

types.name: SellingPlanAllocation
types.description: Represents an association between a variant and a selling plan. Selling plan allocations describe the options offered for each variant, and the price of the variant when purchased with a selling plan.

fields.name: priceAdjustments
fields.description: A list of price adjustments, with a maximum of two. When there are two, the first price adjustment goes into effect at the time of purchase, while the second one starts after a certain number of orders. A price adjustment represents how a selling plan affects pricing when a variant is purchased with a selling plan. Prices display in the customer's currency if the shop is configured for it.

fields.name: sellingPlan
fields.description: A representation of how products and variants can be sold and purchased. For example, an individual selling plan could be '6 weeks of prepaid granola, delivered weekly'.

types.name: SellingPlanAllocationPriceAdjustment
types.description: The resulting prices for variants when they're purchased with a specific selling plan.

fields.name: perDeliveryPrice
fields.description: The effective price for a single delivery. For example, for a prepaid subscription plan that includes 6 deliveries at the price of $48.00, the per delivery price is $8.00.

fields.name: price
fields.description: The price of the variant when it's purchased with a selling plan For example, for a prepaid subscription plan that includes 6 deliveries of $10.00 granola, where the customer gets 20% off, the price is 6 x $10.00 x 0.80 = $48.00.

types.name: Shop
types.description:
Information about the store, including the store's timezone setting
and custom data stored in [metafields](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data).

fields.name: localTime
fields.description:
The current time based on the
[store's timezone setting](https://help.shopify.com/manual/intro-to-shopify/initial-setup/setup-business-settings).

fields.name: metafield
fields.description:
A [custom field](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data) that stores additional information
about a Shopify resource, such as products, orders, and
[many more](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/MetafieldOwnerType).
Using [metafields with Shopify Functions](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/functions/input-output/metafields-for-input-queries)
enables you to customize the checkout experience.

args.name: namespace
args.description:
A category that organizes a group of metafields. Namespaces are used to prevent naming conflicts
between different apps or different parts of the same app. If omitted, then the
[app-reserved namespace](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/ownership)
is used.

args.name: key
args.description:
The unique identifier for the metafield within its namespace. A metafield is composed of a
namespace and a key, in the format `namespace.key`.

types.name: String
types.description: Represents textual data as UTF-8 character sequences. This type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: TimeWithoutTimezone
types.description:
A subset of the [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format that includes the time but not the date or timezone which is determined from context.
For example, "05:43:21".

types.name: Void
types.description: A void type that can be used to return a null value from a mutation.

types.name: WeightUnit
types.description: Units of measurement for weight.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name and description, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields` and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted in [Markdown](https://daringfireball.net/projects/markdown/).

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Included when true.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Requires that exactly one field must be supplied and that field must not be `null`.

directives.name: scaleLimits
directives.description: Scale the Functions resource limits based on the field's length.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Skipped when true.

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.
